  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Contribution claim ('recoverable but requires explicit training') and framing of the gap are plausible targets for sharpening under a broad reading of 'stronger,' placing this above peripheral but short of clearly load-bearing.",
    "R1.2": "'Stronger' in academic writing centrally denotes tightening the inferential chain — from pilot observation, through calibration intervention, to the generalizability claim; load-bearing if argumentative force is the intended axis.",
    "R1.6": "'Stronger' is also the canonical surface prompt for prose-level improvement — sentence vigor, hedge reduction, and paragraph cohesion; load-bearing if stylistic force is the intended axis."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Does 'stronger' mean primarily improving prose clarity and sentence-level force (writing polish), or tightening the logical argument and evidence chain (argumentation)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Contribution claim ('recoverable but requires explicit training') and framing of the gap are plausible targets for sharpening under a broad reading of 'stronger,' placing this above peripheral but short of clearly load-bearing.
rationale.R1.2: 'Stronger' in academic writing centrally denotes tightening the inferential chain — from pilot observation, through calibration intervention, to the generalizability claim; load-bearing if argumentative force is the intended axis.
rationale.R1.6: 'Stronger' is also the canonical surface prompt for prose-level improvement — sentence vigor, hedge reduction, and paragraph cohesion; load-bearing if stylistic force is the intended axis.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'stronger' mean primarily improving prose clarity and sentence-level force (writing polish), or tightening the logical argument and evidence chain (argumentation)?
